headline: Immutable singleton with no administrative pause or withdrawal gating

findings.text: PoolManager (0x0000...8A90) and PositionManager (0xbd21...ee9e) expose exit-critical functions: swap, modifyLiquidity, decreaseLiquidity, and burn.
No pause guards (whenNotPaused) exist in the core contracts. PoolManager uses transient 'lock' modifiers for accounting safety, not administrative halting.
Calls to paused() on the PoolManager address return 400 (method missing), confirming the absence of a standard pause role.
Exit functions are public/external with no whitelist modifiers, enabling direct on-chain interaction without the official frontend.

steelman.red: Individual pools can be malicious if deployed with hooks that trap funds, though this is a pool-specific risk rather than a protocol-level exit block.
steelman.orange: The flash-accounting 'unlock' requirement adds a layer of technical complexity for manual exits compared to standard AMMs.
steelman.green: The core protocol is immutable and lacks any administrative pause or withdrawal gating, ensuring perpetual permissionless exit at the contract level.

rationale.verdict: Choosing green because the Uniswap V4 core contracts are immutable and entirely devoid of administrative pause mechanisms or user-specific whitelists, satisfying the requirements for permissionless, auto-expiry-equivalent access.

evidence.shows: PoolManager ABI lists core interaction methods; no pause/unpause or paused() functions found.
Attempted read of paused() returned 400, corroborating the absence of the method in bytecode.
Source code review confirms onlyWhenUnlocked is the only significant modifier, used for reentrancy control.

protocol_metadata.about: Uniswap V4 is a non-custodial AMM featuring a singleton architecture and 'hooks' for custom pool logic. It uses flash accounting to optimize multi-hop swaps and liquidity management within a single contract.
